steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that scholars claim egocentric temporal reference is universal — that the Yupno, like all humans, use body-based left/right directions to represent past and future. To undermine this claim, we need evidence that the Yupno do not use a body-based left/right system.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The Yupno use their left hand to point to the right when talking about the future and use their left hand to point to the left when talking about the past.: The Yupno still use left/right spatial directions — the directions are just reversed from the norm. This is still an egocentric, body-based frame; it does not undermine the claim that the Yupno use egocentric reference. ✗
  • Choice B — The Yupno always use their left hand to point to the right or the left when denoting future or past events.: Again, the Yupno are still using left/right directional pointing from their own body. This supports, rather than undermines, egocentric reference. ✗
  • Choice C — When referring to the future with backs towards the top of a hill, the Yupno point backward; when referring to the future while standing toward the top of a hill, the Yupno point to the front.: The direction the Yupno point (forward or backward) depends on their orientation relative to the hill, not on a fixed left/right body axis. This is an allocentric (landmark-based) frame — the opposite of egocentric — which directly undermines the claim. ✓
  • Choice D — When referring to the future, the Yupno shake their heads to the left; when referring to the past, the Yupno shake their heads to the right.: Head-shaking left and right is still a body-centered spatial gesture. It does not contradict the core claim that the Yupno use body-based spatial reference for time. ✗
That is the correct answer.
